title: sessions_to_watch() SQL filter: hardcoded states create silent omission risk
description:
Location: wallet/src/db.rs — sessions_to_watch() hardcodes state IN ('address_provided', 'sent'). If a new PaymentState variant representing 'awaiting confirmation' is added later, it won't be included in the watcher unless a developer also updates this SQL string.

The code compiles cleanly on enum change. No test will catch the omission unless a test specifically inserts a session in the new state and calls sessions_to_watch().

Fix: Add a comment above the SQL explaining: 'These are the states that mean the payee is waiting for on-chain confirmation. Any new PaymentState representing in-flight confirmation must be added here.' This makes the invariant visible to the next contributor.
close_reason: Invariant comment added above sessions_to_watch() SQL filter
